CGAL::Greene_convex_decomposition_2.name: CGAL::Greene_convex_decomposition_2
CGAL::Greene_convex_decomposition_2.brief: The Greene_convex_decomposition_2 class implements the approximation algorithm of Greene for the decomposition of an input polygon into convex sub-polygons [6] .
CGAL::Greene_convex_decomposition_2.detailed: This algorithm takes $O(n \log n)$ time and $O(n)$ space, where $ n$ is the size of the input polygon, and outputs a decomposition whose size is guaranteed to be no more than four times the size of the optimal decomposition. Kernel must be a geometric kernel that can be used for the polygon. Container must be a container that can be used for the polygon. It is by default std::vector<typename Kernel::Point_2> . Is model of PolygonConvexDecomposition_2 CGAL::greene_approx_convex_partition_2()

CGAL::Hertel_Mehlhorn_convex_decompositi.name: CGAL::Hertel_Mehlhorn_convex_decomposition_2
CGAL::Hertel_Mehlhorn_convex_decompositi.brief: The Hertel_Mehlhorn_convex_decomposition_2 class implements the approximation algorithm of Hertel and Mehlhorn for decomposing a polygon into convex sub-polygons [8] .
CGAL::Hertel_Mehlhorn_convex_decompositi.detailed: This algorithm constructs a triangulation of the input polygon and proceeds by removing unnecessary triangulation edges. Given the triangulation, the algorithm requires $O(n)$ time and space to construct a convex decomposition (where $ n$ is the size of the input polygon), whose size is guaranteed to be no more than four times the size of the optimal decomposition. Kernel must be a geometric kernel that can be used for the polygon. Container must be a container that can be used for the polygon. It is by default std::vector<typename Kernel::Point_2> . Is model of PolygonConvexDecomposition_2 CGAL::approx_convex_partition_2()

CGAL::Optimal_convex_decomposition_2.name: CGAL::Optimal_convex_decomposition_2
CGAL::Optimal_convex_decomposition_2.brief: The Optimal_convex_decomposition_2 class provides an implementation of Greene's dynamic programming algorithm for optimal decomposition of a polygon into convex sub-polygons [6] .
CGAL::Optimal_convex_decomposition_2.detailed: Note that this algorithm requires $O(n^4)$ time and $O(n^3)$ space in the worst case, where $ n$ is the size of the input polygon. Kernel must be a geometric kernel that can be used for the polygon. Container must be a container that can be used for the polygon. It is by default std::vector<typename Kernel::Point_2> . Is model of PolygonConvexDecomposition_2 CGAL::optimal_convex_partition_2()

CGAL::Polygon_nop_decomposition_2.name: CGAL::Polygon_nop_decomposition_2
CGAL::Polygon_nop_decomposition_2.brief: The Polygon_nop_decomposition_2 class implements a convex decomposition of a polygon, which merely passes the input polygon to the list of output convex polygons.
CGAL::Polygon_nop_decomposition_2.detailed: It should be used when it is known that the input polygon is convex to start with. Is model of PolygonConvexDecomposition_2

CGAL::Polygon_triangulation_decompositio.name: CGAL::Polygon_triangulation_decomposition_2
CGAL::Polygon_triangulation_decompositio.brief: The Polygon_triangulation_decomposition_2 class implements a convex decomposition of a polygon or a polygon with holes into triangles using the Delaunay constrained triangulation functionality of the 2D Triangulation package.

CGAL::Polygon_vertical_decomposition_2.name: CGAL::Polygon_vertical_decomposition_2
CGAL::Polygon_vertical_decomposition_2.brief: The Polygon_vertical_decomposition_2 class implements a convex decomposition of a polygon or a polygon with holes into pseudo trapezoids utilizing the CGAL::decompose() free function of the 2D Arrangements package.
CGAL::Polygon_vertical_decomposition_2.detailed: The algorithm operates in $O(n \log n)$ time and takes $O(n)$ space at the worst case, where $ n$ is the size of the input polygon. Is model of PolygonWithHolesConvexDecomposition_2

CGAL::Small_side_angle_bisector_decompos.name: CGAL::Small_side_angle_bisector_decomposition_2
CGAL::Small_side_angle_bisector_decompos.brief: The Small_side_angle_bisector_decomposition_2 class implements a simple yet efficient heuristic for decomposing an input polygon into convex sub-polygons.
CGAL::Small_side_angle_bisector_decompos.detailed: It is based on the algorithm suggested by Flato and Halperin [5] , but without introducing Steiner points. The algorithm operates in two major steps. In the first step, it tries to subdivide the polygon by connect two reflex vertices with an edge. When this is not possible any more, it eliminates the reflex vertices one by one by connecting them to other convex vertices, such that the new edge best approximates the angle bisector of the reflex vertex. The algorithm operates in $O(n^2)$ time and takes $O(n)$ space at the worst case, where $ n$ is the size of the input polygon. Is model of PolygonConvexDecomposition_2

PolygonConvexDecomposition_2.name: PolygonConvexDecomposition_2
PolygonConvexDecomposition_2.brief: A model of the PolygonConvexDecomposition_2 concept is capable of decomposing an input polygon $ P$ into a set of convex sub-polygons $ P_1, \ldots, P_k$ , such that $ \cup_{i=1}^{k}{P_k} = P$ .
PolygonConvexDecomposition_2.detailed: Has models CGAL::Small_side_angle_bisector_decomposition_2 < Kernel , Container > CGAL::Optimal_convex_decomposition_2 < Kernel , Container > CGAL::Hertel_Mehlhorn_convex_decomposition_2 < Kernel , Container > CGAL::Greene_convex_decomposition_2 < Kernel , Container > CGAL::Polygon_nop_decomposition_2 < Kernel , Container >

members.name: Container
members.detailed: By default it is std::vector<typename Kernel::Point_2> .

members.name: Point_2
members.brief: the point type, used to represent polygon vertices.

members.name: Polygon_2
members.brief: the polygon type, defined as Polygon_2 < Kernel , Container > .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: decomposes the input polygon P into convex sub-polygons, and writes them to the output iterator oi .
members.detailed: The value-type of the output iterator must be Polygon_2 . The function returns a past-the-end iterator for the convex sub-polygons.

PolygonWithHolesConvexDecomposition_2.name: PolygonWithHolesConvexDecomposition_2
PolygonWithHolesConvexDecomposition_2.brief: A model of the PolygonWithHolesConvexDecomposition_2 concept is capable of decomposing an input polygon $ P$ , which may have holes, into a set of convex sub-polygons $ P_1, \ldots, P_k$ , such that $ \cup_{i=1}^{k}{P_k} = P$ .

members.name: Polygon_with_holes_2
members.brief: the polygon with holes type, defined as Polygon_with_holes_2 <Kernel,Container> .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: decomposes the input polygon with holes P into convex sub-polygons, and writes them to the output iterator oi .
members.detailed: The value-type of the output iterator must be Polygon_2 . The function returns a past-the-end iterator for the convex sub-polygons.
